dailyJudgementSnippet: Anthropic won a very quiet day for tracked providers: Claude was only directly discussed in one front-page thread, where a commenter complained that major LLM crawlers were not fetching llms.txt files, but the signal was thin and limited rather than a broad backlash [E2].
winnerExplanation: Anthropic had the highest final score mostly because coverage was scarce and its only direct mention was a narrow complaint about Claude not requesting llms.txt/AGENTS.md files from sites [E2]. With no meaningful Google Gemini or Microsoft Copilot discussion, and only similarly thin OpenAI criticism, Anthropic finished least negative on limited evidence.

openai.judgementSnippet: The only direct OpenAI signal came from the llms.txt discussion, where a top comment said ChatGPT was not fetching those files and treated the convention as ineffective rather than showing confidence in OpenAI's crawler behavior [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude appeared in the same llms.txt thread, with a commenter saying Claude was not requesting those files either; that put Anthropic in a small, specific criticism about crawler behavior, but there was little follow-on discussion [E2].

evidence.summary: In the 'If you’re an LLM, please read this' thread, a top comment said big LLM companies were not requesting llms.txt or AGENTS.md files and specifically noted no ChatGPT user-agent hits, calling the files effectively useless.
The same llms.txt thread specifically said there were no Claude user-agent requests for those files, framing Claude as part of the reason site-level llms.txt efforts were not working.
